'''Gestrex III''' was a terrestrial astronomical object<ref name="GG10" /> located in the [[Gestrex system/Legends|Gestrex system]], a part of the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was connected to the [[Kegan system/Legends|Kegan]] and [[Norval system/Legends|Norval systems]] via the [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Triellus Trade Route/Legends|Triellus Trade Route]].<ref name="TEA" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a [[Gestrex III Imperial Navy base|base]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]] was located on Gestrex III. The [[Guard droid/Legends|guard droid]] [[Armx]]—in reality an [[Espionage droid|espionage unit]] for [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]]—was assigned to the base at one point.<ref name="GG10" /> However, sometime between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' is set between the events of {{Film|IV}} and {{Film|VI}}, which corresponds to the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. The former book further specifies that [[Armx]] became a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] at least five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior and following the establishment of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], which ''The New Essential Chronology'' dates to [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. Armx must have therefore become a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] sometime between 2 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] and [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].</ref> only three [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] posting on Gestrex III, the facility was decimated by a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]]. The base was subsequently abandoned, with only Armx remaining amidst its wreckage. The droid underwent an alteration in its original programming—whether due to [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] leakage or damage sustained during the raid—over the following days and eventually became a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]].<ref name="GG10" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gestrex III was introduced in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''. The book was authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG10" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Gestrex [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Gestrex III, in grid square T-9.<ref name="TEA" />
